observations.managementAction: The Management has reorganized the Inventory Committee tasked to conduct the physical count. Preliminary activities for the physical count have been conducted.
observations.reason:
The RPCLRN has been submitted to the Audit Team however, it bears incomplete details about the road networks.

Not Implemented

The Supply Officer has not prepared and submitted the required reports.

Implemented

Report on the cost segregation of road components was submitted by the Municipal Engineer as evidenced by the RPCLRN prepared by the Supply Officer.

Not Implemented

Accounting records still includes road networks which are not owned by the Municipality. Moreover, no disclosure was made on the details of the road networks of the Municipality.

Reiterated in finding number 1 in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

require the Supply Officer to religiously prepare RIS for all issuances of supplies and materials, consolidate the aforementioned forms to be attached in the preparation of SSMI and subsequently submit the reports to the Accounting Unit;



instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary JEVs to record actual issuances, based on the submitted SSMI and RIS, and reflect issuances to its respective inventory accounts; and



require both the Supply Office and Accounting Office to consistently reconcile actual inventories on stock and inventory accounting records in order to achieve the computation of accurate balances and the timely discovery and correction of errors.
observations.managementAction: The Management has informed all individuals concerned to coordinate with the Supply Officer to facilitate the immediate preparation of RIS, as the basis in accomplishing the SSMI.
observations.reason:
No substantial compliance was made as the Inventories Held for Distribution accounts still includes those deliveries made in 2023 and prior years.

Not Implemented

Several adjustments were made in CY 2024 to recognize the distribution, however, a huge amount remained on record.

Not Implemented

Records between the 2 offices remains unreconciled due to the non-submission of SSMI to the Accounting Office as basis in updating the records of the latter.

Reiterated in finding number 2 in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned employee refund the overpayment of salaries and other benefits.
observations.managementAction: The employee concerned has not started the refund.
observations.reason: No refund has been made as of this writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the concerned employees to undertake all other procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee has been re-organized who shall conduct the comprehensive physical count of PPE.
observations.reason: The Report of Physical Count of PPEs has not been completed as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the Supply Office to finalize the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) as basis in recording the dropping from the books the unserviceable properties carried in the PPE accounts;


direct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to evaluate the reasonable appraisal value of the unserviceable properties listed in the IIRUP and promptly cause the sale of these properties pursuant to the directive of Section 79 of PD No. 1445, and in accordance with the procedures cited in the DBM Manual on Disposal of Government Property and COA Circular No. 89-296; and



advise the concerned personnel to submit the IIRUP to the Accounting Office upon completion of the disposal activity as it will be the basis for adjusting the balances of the affected PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has re-organized the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count.


The Appraisal and Disposal Committee has determined the value of the unserviceable properties for disposal.


The concerned personnel committed to accomplish the IIRUP the soonest.
observations.reason:
No IIRUP has been finalized as basis in derecognizing the unserviceable properties from the books.

Not Implemented

The subject unserviceable properties were not yet disposed.


Not Implemented

No adjustment was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management trace from the related documents to identify the name of the beneficiaries of the various capital assistance and to exert extra effort in collecting the funds lent out by sending demand letters to concerned persons so other bona fide beneficiaries within the municipality could also avail of the program.
observations.managementAction: The Management has sent notifications to the beneficiaries demanding their repayments for the loan granted to them.
observations.reason: Of the total amount of uncollected loans, the Management was able to collect ₱160,298.00 in previous years thus leaving a balance of ₱142,508.00 uncollected loans.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
require the requisitioning offices and the Supply Officer to trace back the issuances made in 2023 and prior years, and immediately prepare the corresponding SSMI to be submitted to the MA;
instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the actual issuance of the inventories based on the SSMI to be submitted by the Supply Officer; and
oblige the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to regularly reconcile their records in order to reflect the accurate balances of inventories as at year-end.

We recommended that the Management stop utilizing government funds on religious activities pursuant to Section 335 of RA No. 7160, instead prioritize activities or programs that can benefit the constituents as a whole.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately process the application for fidelity bond of the Supply Officer commensurate with his accountabilities to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended that the Management immediately facilitate the full integration of barangay clearance fee/s’ collection in the Municipality’s system by requiring the concerned Barangays to execute an agreement with the Municipality stating therein their respective duties and obligations on the collection of barangay clearance fee/s in order to expedite the processing of application and renewal of permits of business owners in the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert to the GF any unutilized balances of the Trust Liability – DRRMF aging more than five years and have the same appropriated for other social services of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has transferred the unutilized balances to the GF.
observations.reason: The transfer was made thru JEV No. 300-24-07-056 dated July 18, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to amend the provisions of the Municipal Revenue Code on the collection of CTC and follow the amounts provided by the Local Government Code (LGC) in order to provide Treasury personnel a uniform guideline in ensuring proper assessment and collection of CTC.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office has shifted their basis in collecting fees for CTC
observations.reason: Post-audit of collections showed that collections for CTCs were already based on the provisions of the LGC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management implement an internal control to properly monitor the status of the street light facilities of the Municipality in order to avoid paying unnecessary expenses for under or non-utilization of the same.



We further recommended, when applicable, to seek for disconnection of any existing line that are no longer necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Management has formulated an internal control to monitor the status of streetlight facilities of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Substantial number of streetlights with minimum electric bill without consumption has been disconnected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

whenever applicable, implement the PPAs listed above as embodied in the Municipal Economic Development Plan (EDP);



revisit the plans and budget embodied in the Municipal EDP and identify the necessities of the listed projects therein, otherwise identify more appropriate and responsive PPAs; and

appropriate the savings of funds derived from previous implementation of projects to new projects that are aligned to the purpose of the 20% DF. These would ensure that benefits intended for the Municipality’s populace will be met.
observations.managementAction: The allocated amount for the unimplemented PPAs identified has been reprogrammed and / or re-appropriated under Municipal Appropriations Ordinance No. 2024-3 dated July 5, 2024.
observations.reason:
In CY 2024, out of the 57 PPAs funded under Current and Continuing Appropriations for 20% DF, 52 PPAs has been implemented during the year.

Implemented

More responsive PPAs listed in the Municipal EDP were identified and funded.

Implemented

The savings derived from previously implemented PPAs were reprogrammed to new PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to correct RPT and SET Receivable based on the Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of December 31, 2022.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has made the adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.reason: Reconciliations and adjustments were made as per JEV No. 10-0131 drawn in October 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management request the Sangguniang Bayan for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for the collection of delinquencies after the submission of the Municipal Treasurer of the certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected with statement therein of the reason for non-collection.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has formulated several mechanisms to collect the delinquent taxes.
observations.reason: Substantial amount has been collected pertaining to the delinquencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management conduct a complete physical inventory of all the assets of the municipality, reconcile the balances with the accounting records and exert all efforts in locating the documents needed to make the necessary adjustments in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The records of the accounting office and the supply office have been reconciled.
observations.reason: Records showed that the subject accounts have been adjusted accordingly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:

Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to correct RPT and SET Receivable based on the Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of December 31, 2022; 

Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis in recording the RPT and SET Receivable; and

Municipal Treasurer to communicate results of new assessments and reassessments during the year with the Municipal Accountant for necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal accountant set up in the books of accounts receivable for RPT and SEF based on the certified list from the Municipal Treasurer. Previous records are being reconciled to determine the correctness of the information stated in therein.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the concerned employee refund the overpayment of salaries and other benefits.

Also, we recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of Section 327 of RA No. 7160 and refrain further disbursements of funds from any of the items of appropriation declared inoperative, disallowed or reduced by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.
observations.managementAction: Management made inquiries to with other regulatory agencies and stopped paying the noted salary.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify its campaign in collecting tax delinquencies; prepare and submit action plan on the legal measures and strategies the Office of the Municipal Treasurer will adopt to improve the collection efficiency on delinquent taxes; and avail of the remedies in collecting delinquent real property taxes provided under Section 256 of RA No. 7160; 

Request the Sangguniang Bayan for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for the collection of delinquencies after the submission of the Municipal Treasurer of the certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected with statement therein of the reason for non-collection; and

Require the OIC Municipal Assessor to come up with a systematic and effective manner of identifying rightful and legal owners of the real properties declared unknown in the Tax Declaration. Likewise, to conduct periodic ocular inspection of real properties in the Municipality in order to determine their actual use for proper classification, valuation and assessment.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted tax campaign during the year by visiting various barangays

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the:

concerned employees to undertake all other procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Inventory taking of PPE was undertaken and report preparation is now on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following courses of action from concerned officials and employees:

The Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO), together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, immediately look into the unimplemented projects under the 20% Development Fund and determine causes of their non-implementation. Formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement development projects. Consider formulating a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented projects;

The offices concerned vigorously pursue the completion of the projects to be implemented the soonest time possible in order to achieve the objectives of the said projects in providing socio-economic benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

The Municipal Engineering Office improve the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of the Program of Work of unimplemented projects. 





The MPDO enhance monitoring of implementation of projects in coordination with the implementing offices and to submit regularly status reports of implementation to the Municipal Mayor for his information so that appropriate measures can be instituted to obtain optimal utilization of fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management programmed PPAs under 20% Local Development Funds totaling ₱73,512,457.80 composed of 65 projects in CY 2022. However, only 28 of these were implemented with a total cost of                    ₱30,109,030.80 or barely 41% of the total budgeted amount.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the Supply Office to finalized the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) as basis in recording the dropping from the books the unserviceable properties carried in the PPE accounts; 


Direct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to evaluate the reasonable appraisal value of the unserviceable properties listed in the IIRUP and promptly cause the sale of these properties pursuant to the directive of Section 79 of PD No. 1445, and in accordance with the procedures cited in the DBM Manual on Disposal of Government Property and COA Circular No. 89-296; and

Advise the concerned personnel to submit the IIRUP to the Accounting Office upon completion of the disposal activity as it will be the basis for adjusting the balances of the affected PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The concerned personnel are in the process of determining the complete list of properties to be included in the IIRUP. These will be finalized in the preparation of required Inventory Reports under COA Circular 2020-006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee that will conduct inventory of the local roads and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have not totally derecognized in the books the Road Networks due for transfer to Barangays e.g. Roads and other related improvements pertaining to Barangay roads.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO Designate to prepare, consolidate and submit the RIS to the Municipal Account which shall be the basis of the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher to record the issuances of inventories.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has received RIS for some of the inventories and shall continue to coordinate with the concerned offices and personnel for the unsubmitted RIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to trace from the related documents to identify the name of the beneficiaries of the various capital assistance and to exert extra effort in collecting the funds lent out by sending demand letters to concerned persons so other bona fide beneficiaries within the municipality could also avail of the program.
observations.managementAction:
Management has collected some of the receivables and shall continue to collect the remaining balance.

Likewise, necessary documents to support the Request for write-off of some of the accounts are being prepared and gathered by the concerned personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the assets of the municipality, reconcile the balances with the accounting records and exert all efforts in locating the documents needed to make the necessary adjustments in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting office is still securing the records as the basis for the dropping of the account from the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to:
require the Supply Officer to religiously prepare RIS for all issuances of supplies and materials, consolidate the aforementioned forms to be attached in the preparation of SSMI and subsequently submit the reports to the Accounting Unit;
instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary JEVs to record actual issuances, based on the submitted SSMI and RIS, and reflect issuances to its respective inventory accounts; and
require both the Supply Office and Accounting Office to consistently reconcile actual inventories on stock and inventory accounting records in order to achieve the computation of accurate balances and the timely discovery and correction of errors.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert to the General Fund any unutilized balances of the Trust Liability – DRRMF aging more than five years and have the same appropriated for other social services of the Municipality.

We recommended management to amend the provisions of the Municipal Revenue Code on the collection of CTC and follow the amounts provided by the Local Government Code in order to provide Treasury personnel a uniform guideline in ensuring proper assessment and collection of CTC.

We recommended the Management to implement an internal control to properly monitor the status of the street light facilities of the Municipality in order to avoid paying unnecessary expenses for under or non-utilization of the same.

We further recommended, when applicable, to seek for disconnection of any existing line that are no longer necessary.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Whenever applicable, implement the PPAs listed above as embodied in the Municipal EDP;
Revisit the plans and budget embodied in the Municipal EDP and identify the necessities of the listed projects therein, otherwise identify more appropriate and responsive PPAs; and
Appropriate the savings of funds derived from previous implementation of projects to new projects that are aligned to the purpose of the 20% DF. These would ensure that benefits intended for the Municipality’s populace will be met.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payment for progress and final billings be made as soon as possible and in no case later than forty-five (45) days after the submission of request for payment as required under Section 4 of the Manual of Procedures for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects.
observations.managementAction: Management took note on the period of processing of documents to meet target time of payment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the submission of As-Built Drawings/Plans in order that changes made during the construction process and the exact rendering of the finished project could be fully ascertained and to support the final payments on infrastructure projects in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Item 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We also recommended that the amount of ₱5,000.00 be withheld for failure to produce the said documents on the required date as set forth in the Special Conditions of Contract.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer had taken steps that the documents noted in this observation are present in prior to payment
The Inventory Committee already completed the physical inventory of 27 accounts of the targeted 33 PPE accounts.
Management had submitted IIRUP for the Unserviceable Property but the schedule for the final inspection of the unserviceable properties was not yet determined.
Management has made changes in their bidding requirements to be clearer to all potential bidders and to be able to determine the most qualified bidder for each procurement.

The Inventory Committee is still in the process of completing the JEVs for the identification of the segregation and transfer of all local roads that were constructed on barangay roads to the books of the barangays. Local roads constructed on national and provincial roads were also identified.

For CY 2021, the municipality had listed various PPAs to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund surmounting to ₱43,553,925.85. Of this amount, a total of P7,180,000.00 remained unimplemented despite availability of funds.
The Municipal Accountant has received RIS for some of the inventories and shall continue to coordinate with the concerned offices and personnel for the unsubmitted RIS.
Management has collected some of the receivables and shall continue to collect the remaining balance.
Management was able to identify the composition of the account and is now in the process of reconciling with the actual inventory count.
Management has already collected some of the receivables and shall continue to collect the remaining balance.

1.6 We recommended that Management direct the:
a. Supply Officer, in coordination with the Inventory Committee to update
the PIP to properly plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the
physical inventory within the soonest possible time.
b. concerned employees to undertake all other procedures prescribed under
COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with the reconciled and correct
balances of PPE accounts.

2.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Supply Officer to
make representation with the PGIN for the cost of the above-mentioned
donated vehicle; and if not available, determine its fair market value, and
furnish the Municipal Accountant copies of the transfer documents as basis
for recording in the books of the Municipality.

make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the disallowed amount given
to the beneficiaries as unliquidated Fund Transfer from DSWD. Also,
require the LGU officials who had given the ESP to ineligible beneficiaries

Lubricants consumed for various Sanjera projects.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant restated the affected accounts
presently the prior submitted restated financial statement as of December 31, 2020 were restated.
The Municipal Engineer prepared the necessary documentary requirements and submitted the same to the Municipal Accountant.
Proper verification of all submitted documents of the bidders with LCB are being done by the BAC and TWG to determine if all the requirements of the bids are met.

The Engineer has been approving requests for suspension or extension of ongoing contracts with valid reasons. The BAC consistently require the CHSP, or at least a proof of application for CHSP as a requirement for the execution of the contract.
The Supply Officer Designate did consistently indicate in the RIS all relevant information of items for issuance.
The Supply Officer made sure that distribution lists were duly signed by the intended recipients.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management abide strictly with the following prescribed controls over PPE as provided for in the pertinent laws, rules and regulations:
a. Create an Inventory Committee/Committee to conduct a complete inventory of all the properties of the Agency at year-end and reconcile results with the corresponding accounting and property records. Discrepancy, if any, should be analyzed/investigated.
b. Require the Inventory Committee to provide tagging of all PPEs (property identification and coding) pursuant to COA Circular No. 80-124 dated January 18, 1980.
c. Instruct the Supply Officer Designate to update/renew all Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) to establish who is in actual possession or custody of all government properties in accordance with Chapter 10, Section 21, of the Government Accounting Manual, Vol. I.
d. Require the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively, for each class of property in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I
e. Require the Supply Officer Designate to exclude small tangible items with serviceable life of more than one year from the RPCPPE, cancel PARs prepared in the issuance of these items and cause the preparation of ICS to replace the PARs.
f. Instruct the different Departments/Offices to report donated properties in their respective offices to the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant for proper recording and accounting in the books.
g. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer Designate to reconcile their records regularly in order to establish the accuracy of balances of each PPE account presented in the financial statement as of a certain period.

Create an Inventory An Inventory Implemented Committee/Committee to conduct a complete inventory of all the constituted by the Management and order of Inventory properties of the Agency at year- Management and Executive Committee was submitted. Supply office end and reconcile results with the corresponding Order was submitted to the COA Office. accounting and property records. Discrepancy, if any, should be issued. Supply office and Accounting office analyzed/investigated. had started setting-up the necessary records to be maintained. The notice of schedule of physical inventory taking dated March 29, 2021 was received by the office on April 30, 2021.
b. Require the Inventory To be undertaken after tagging of the completion of the Committee to provide PPEs (property identification inventory taking. The conduct of Physical tagging of all PPEs pursuant to COA Circular No. 80-124 dated January 18, 1980. Inventory is ongoing with 50% completion.
c. Instruct the Supply Officer To be undertaken after Designate to update/renew all Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) to establish who is in actual possession or custody of all government properties in accordance with Chapter 10, Section 21, of the Government Accounting Manual, Vol. I. the completion of the inventory taking. The conduct of Physical Inventory is ongoing with 50% completion.
d. Require the Supply Officer To be undertaken after and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively, for each class of property in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I the completion of the inventory taking. The conduct of Physical Inventory is ongoing with 50% completion.
e. Require the Supply Officer To be undertaken after Designate to exclude small tangible items with serviceable life of more than one year from the RPCPPE, cancel PARs prepared in the issuance of these items and cause the preparation of ICS to replace the PARs. the completion of the inventory taking. The conduct of Physical Inventory is ongoing with 50% completion.
f. Instruct the different Municipal Departments/Offices to report donated properties in their respective offices to the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant for proper recording and accounting in the books. 2019-018 re: An Ordinance to strengthen the internal control over the management, accounting and recording of all supplies and equipment received from outside sources for the Municipality of Vintar, Ilocos Norte was furnished the Audit Team.
g. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer Designate to reconcile their records regularly in order to establish the accuracy of balances of each PPE account presented in the financial statement as of a certain period. To be undertaken after the completion of the inventory taking. The conduct of Physical Inventory is ongoing with 50% completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC/TWG to carefully and meticulously verify, validate and ascertain all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid during post-qualification pursuant to Section 34.3.b.i of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure observance and attainment of the principle on competitiveness.
observations.managementAction: Management has made changes in their bidding requirements to be clearer to all potential bidders and to be able to determine the most qualified bidder for each procurement.
observations.reason: Post-audit of transactions revealed that Management still procured various goods to suppliers who do not meet the eligibility requirements for the specific transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee that will conduct inventory of the local roads and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction: Management created the Inventory Committee and started to account all local roads. The Municipal Accountant have received RIS for some of the inventories and shall continue to coordinate with the concerned offices and personnel for the unsubmitted RIS.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2020, a total of 207 Road Networks totalling ₱114,848,440.49 were dropped from the books and transferred to Barangays. The Inventory Committee is still in the process of completing the identification of Road Networks to be maintained in the books of the LGU.

observations.managementAction:
The Management was able to implement majority of its projects under the 20% Development fund. Out of the 16 projects programmed for CY 2020 with a total budgeted amount of ₱44,251,337.02, only 5 projects totaling ₱4,900,000.00 were not implemented.
Management was able to complete majority of its development projects despite the present Covid-19 pandemic situation. However, 11.07% of its projects remained unimplemented.
Despite the improved planning of work, the volume of projects funded from various sources being catered by the Engineering office affected the scheduling of work activities in implementing the projects.
MPDO failed to regularly monitor the project implementation.
observations.reason: Management failed to effectively strategize the implementation of development projects. The MPDO failed to regularly monitor the project implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO Designate to prepare, consolidate and submit the RIS to the Municipal Accountant which shall be the basis of the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher to record the issuances of inventories.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has received RIS for some of the inventories and shall continue to coordinate with the concerned offices and personnel for the unsubmitted RIS.
observations.reason: Adjusting entries were already taken by the Accounting office for the received RIS. For CY 2020, the balances of inventory held for distribution were reduced by ₱514,830.00.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Management to trace from the related documents to identify the name of the beneficiaries of the various capital assistance and to exert extra effort in collecting the funds lent out by sending demand letters to concerned persons so other bona fide beneficiaries within the municipality could also avail of the program.
observations.managementAction: Management has collected some of the receivables and shall continue to collect the remaining balance. Request for the write-off of some of the accounts are also being considered upon the establishment of valid and acceptable reasons.
observations.reason: During CY 2020, Management was able to collect ₱1,200.00 of the loan assistance. Management is exerting efforts to trace from the related documents to identify the name of the beneficiaries of the various capital assistance.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated the Management to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the assets of the municipality, reconcile the balances with the accounting records and exert all efforts in locating the documents needed to make the necessary adjustments in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to identify the composition of the account and is now in the process of reconciling with the actual inventory count.
observations.reason: Necessary adjustment was not yet made because of the on-going reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that collection of receivables must be intensified so that amount could be used to finance priority projects of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management already collected some of the receivables and shall continue to collect the remaining balance. Management might propose for the write-off of some of the accounts in the future after establishing valid and acceptable reasons to propose such.
observations.reason: As at December 31, 2020, Due from Officers and Employees balance is ₱30,307.21.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating
the prior year balances of the affected accounts pursuant to pertinent provisions
of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that Management require the:
a. OIC - Municipal Engineer to submit periodically a list of completed
projects with the necessary documentary requirements to the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate proper recording/treatment in the books; and
b. Municipal Accountant to reclassify the completed projects from CIP
account to the appropriate PPE account based on the Certificate of 100%
Completion issued by the Engineering Office and recognize the
depreciation of reclassified assets from the time these projects were
completed to the present with the following proposed journal entries:
Dr Appropriate PPE Account 29,947,774.62
Cr Construction in Progress 29,947,774.62
(the closure of CIP for completed projects)
Dr Prior Period Adjustment 638,113.71
Cr Accumulated Depreciation 638,113.71
(to record the depreciation of unrecognized PPE account in CY 2019)
Dr Prior Period Adjustment 1,441,641.21
Cr Accumulated Depreciation 1,441,641.21
(to record the depreciation for CY 2020)

observations.managementAction:
Management had submitted IIRUP for CY 2019 but the schedule for the final inspection at the safekeeping of the unserviceable properties was not yet determined. Additional storage space was provided for the safekeeping of unserviceable properties.
Preparation of necessary documents for the ultimate disposal of the unserviceable properties is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to capitalize the cost of major repairs to present the correct amount of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management already capitalized the cost of repairs. A Journal Entry Voucher was prepared and recorded in the books to effect the necessary adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and GSO Designate to update their records and derecognize the properties issued to public schools in the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management already prepared the list of properties as well as the necessary documents for their turn-over to public schools. Properties with a total net book value of ₱660,042.19 were already dropped from the books and transferred to public schools as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 specifically on the payment of Honoraria to lecturers and resource persons to avoid disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office claimed that supporting documents of payrolls on Honoraria paid to lecturers and resource persons are being required when they are presented to be processed. Proofs on this action were already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 specifically Item IV.B of Annex H – prohibition on the splitting of government contracts, to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management has made amendments in the preparation and presentation in PPMPs and APPs with regards to projects that were similar to the specific cited project to clearly present how the LGU will implement these projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to Section 62.1 of RA No. 9184 to avoid loss of government funds in case of uncorrected manufacturing defects by the supplier. The management shall create a policy requiring warranty security for all procurement of items/goods, depending on their nature.
observations.managementAction: Management already required warranty security for procurement projects conducted through Alternative Methods. Warranty Security on goods procured were no longer deducted from those paid beyond the 90 days warranty period from the date of AIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that corresponding withholding taxes are deducted from the payments of services in accordance with pertinent tax rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management is now requiring individuals who have been hired for specific services with payment of more than ₱10,000.00 to be registered with the BIR so that corresponding taxes could be deducted and eventually remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management be cautious in the implementation of its programs and projects specifically in the distribution of assistance to various recipients to ensure that these are duly supported with appropriate documents.
observations.managementAction: Official Report from the MDRRMO were presented to support the distribution of Portland cement to Sanjeras. Likewise, documented evaluation reports to be accomplished by the Department who initiated the project were already attached as supporting documents in the preparation of Disbursement Vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management come up with the final list of properties for various insurance with the GSIS and comply with the provisions of the Property Insurance Law.
observations.managementAction: Has already insured the various equipment and facilities and buildings of the LGU.
observations.reason: The LGU paid a total of ₱82,973.54 to GSIS for the insurance coverage of its 12 buildings on January 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management strictly comply with Section 54.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 76-41 to ensure transparency, economy and efficiency in all its procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: Management consolidated their procurements during the year.
observations.reason: Post-audit of transactions proved that Management had consolidated their procurements for the year.

observations.managementAction:
Management already returned the residual PDAF. Per review of accounting records, the adjusted balances of Unutilized PDAF and other trust receipts amounting to ₱390,140.73 and ₱264,915.20, respectively, were returned to the Bureau of the Treasury or source agencies as follows:
Unutilized PDAF
July 2020 - ₱390,140.73
Unutilized other trust receipts

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to adhere to section 25.2b Item viii (3) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management attached the contractor’s list of major equipment units required in the infrastructural projects.
observations.reason: Auditorial review of various projects showed that Management observed section 25.2b Item viii (3) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management abide strictly with the following
prescribed controls over PPE as provided for in the pertinent laws, rules and
regulations:

a. Create an Inventory Team/Committee to conduct a complete inventory
of all the properties of the Agency at year-end and reconcile results with
the accounting and property records. Discrepancy, if any, should be
analyzed/investigated.
b. Require the Inventory Committee to provide tagging of all PPEs
(property identification and coding) pursuant to COA Circular No. 80-
124 dated January 18, 1980.
c. Instruct the Supply Officer Designate to update/renew all Property
Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) to establish who is in actual
possession or custody of all government properties in accordance with
Chapter 10, Section 21, of the Government Accounting Manual, Vol. I.
d. Require the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain
Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively, for each class
of property in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on
the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units,
Volume I
e. Require the Supply Officer Designate to exclude small tangible items
with serviceable life of more than one year from the RPCPPE, cancel
PARs prepared in the issuance of these items and cause the preparation
of ICS to replace the PARs.
f. Instruct the different Departments/Offices to report donated properties
in their respective offices to the Supply Officer and Municipal
Accountant for proper recording and accounting in the books.
g. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer Designate to
reconcile their records regularly in order to establish the accuracy of
balances of each PPE account presented in the financial statement as of
a certain period.

We recommended that Management:

a. Provide enough storage space wherein unserviceable properties could
be properly secured and safeguarded against the elements and instruct
all offices to surrender their reported unserviceable properties in
preparation for their ultimate disposal;
b. Direct the Supply Office to finalized the Inspection and Inventory
Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) as basis in recording the
dropping from the books the unserviceable properties carried in the
PPE accounts;
c. Direct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to evaluate the reasonable
appraisal value of the unserviceable properties listed in the IIRUP and
promptly cause the sale of these properties pursuant to the directive of
Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445, and in accordance with the procedures
cited in the DBM Manual on Disposal of Government Property and
COA Circular No. 89-296; and
d. Advise the concerned personnel to submit the IIRUP to the Accounting
Office upon completion of the disposal activity as it will be the basis for
adjusting the balances of the affected PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
capitalize the cost of major repairs to present the correct amount of the
Property, Plant and Equipment accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and
GSO Designate to update their records and derecognize the properties issued to
public schools in the municipality.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the provisions of COA
Circular 2012-001 specifically on the payment of Honoraria to lecturers and
resource persons to avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of
R.A. No. 9184 specifically Item IV.B of Annex H - prohibition on the splitting of
government contracts, to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management require the BAC/TWG to carefully
and meticulously verify, validate and ascertain all statements made and
documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid during post-
qualification pursuant to Section 34.3.b.i of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No.
9184 to ensure observance and attainment of the principle on competitiveness.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to Section 62.1 of R.A.
No. 9184 to avoid loss of government funds in case of uncorrected
manufacturing defects by the supplier.

We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee that will conduct inventory of the local roads and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 created the Inventory Committee and started to account all local roads.
observations.reason: √ Inventory Committee created, however, the report of Physical Count of local road network as at December 31, 2019 was not submitted for audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management come up with the final list of properties for insurance with the GSIS and comply with the provisions of The Property Insurance Law.
observations.managementAction: Management had insured the various equipment and buildings of the LGU.
observations.reason: √ As at December 31, 2019, facilities and equipment were already insured while Buildings are still in process with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations to effect the legality of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management had directed all the concerned departments to monitor the progress of their purchase of gasoline. The LGU now have complete documents and contract before any requisition from the supplier is made.
observations.reason: √ The LGU now have complete documents and contract before any requisition from the supplier is made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
1. Ensure compliance of the aforementioned provision of the Government Procurement Manual Volume 1, and
2. Evaluate the necessity of hiring additional work force to augment the personnel in the BAC Secretariat.
observations.managementAction: Management had made the necessary adjustments to address and adhere to the observations and recommendations.
observations.reason: √ The stated observations and recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with Section 54.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 76-41 to ensure transparency, economy and efficiency in all its procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: Management had consolidated majority of the procurements during the year.
observations.reason: √ For CY 2019, majority of the procurement projects being undertaken already complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.managementAction: Management had returned a portion of the residual PDAF in CY 2019.
observations.reason: √ Review of accounting records showed a decrease in the residual PDAF and other receipts amounting to P165,804.26.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and BIR Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13, 2014 on the prescribed documentary requirements to support transactions on payment of purchases.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted the required sales invoices.
observations.reason: √ For CY 2019 transactions, the required documents were required from the suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from paying registration fees directly to the hotel venue and require official receipts from the sponsoring agency or group for complete documentation of government expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Management had informed the audit team that the management scheme was set by the Housing and Land Use Regulatory Board (HLURB) and not by the participants from the LGU-Vintar. These are the only trainings that required the payment of registration fees to the hotel. Nonetheless, management agreed to comply with our recommendation to refrain from paying registration fee directly to the hotel management.
observations.reason: √ Post audit proves the stated action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe the above provision of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA. 9184 on the coverage of guarantee bonds in lieu of retention money to assure the Government of the indemnity.
observations.managementAction: Management had required from the contractors the correct and proper form of bond to replace the retention money.
observations.reason: √ The stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Supply Officer to prepare a RPCPPE by type as provided in the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units and submit the same to COA office for verification.
observations.managementAction: Supply officer had submitted the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2019 grouped by type.
observations.reason: √ Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management post every Notice of Award of procurement in the PhilGEPS website within the period provided by law to achieve transparency and to avoid any doubts on the propriety of bidding activity.
observations.managementAction: Management posted all NOA within the reglementary period.
observations.reason: √ Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
a. The Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO), together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, immediately look into the unimplemented projects under the 20% Development Fund and determine causes of their non-implementation. Formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement development projects. Consider formulating a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented projects;
b. The offices concerned vigorously pursue the completion of the projects to be implemented the soonest time possible in order to achieve the objectives of the said projects in providing socio-economic benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
c. The Municipal Engineering Office improve the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of the Program of Work of unimplemented projects.
d. The MPDO enhance monitoring of implementation of projects in coordination with the implementing offices and to submit regularly status reports of implementation to the Municipal Mayor for his information so that appropriate measures can be instituted to obtain optimal utilization of fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to implement majority of its projects under the 20% Development Fund for CY 2019 with only seven (7) out of seventy-five (75) projects not implemented.
observations.reason: √ Review of the 20% Development Fund utilization report proves the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO Designate to prepare, consolidate and submit the RIS to the Municipal Account which shall be the basis of the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher to record the issuances of inventories.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had received RIS for some of the inventories and received RIS. The unsubmitted RIS shall continue to coordinate with the concerned offices and personnel for the unsubmitted RIS.
observations.reason: The balances of inventory held for distribution were reduced by P 3,093,401.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to adhere to section 25.2b Item viii (3) of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management had attached the contractor’s list of major equipment units required in the infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: √ Auditorial Contract Review Analysis conducted on the Infrastructure projects of the Agency revealed that the attached contractor’s list of major equipment units for some projects were still not fully supported.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that all offices shall prepare and submit Project Procurement Management Plan to the BAC Secretariat for them to prepare the consolidated Annual Procurement Plan yearly and so that all procurement on goods, services and infrastructure projects will be properly planned.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted their Annual Procurement Plan to COA Office.
observations.reason: √ Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the management to trace from the related documents to identify the name of the beneficiaries of the various capital assistance and to exert extra effort in collecting the funds lent out by sending demand letters to concerned persons so other bona fide beneficiaries within the municipality could also avail of the program.
observations.managementAction: Management had collected some of the receivables and shall continue to collect the remaining balance. Request for the write-off of some of the accounts are also being considered upon establishing valid and acceptable reasons.
observations.reason: During CY 2019, Management was able to collect P4,200.00 of the remaining balance. Management is still in the process of collecting the amount due from the various capital assistance.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated the management to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the assets of the municipality, reconcile the balances with the accounting records and exert all efforts in locating the documents needed to make the necessary adjustments in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to identify the composition of the account and is now in the process of reconciling with the actual inventory count.
observations.reason: Necessary adjustment was not yet made because of the on-going reconciliation of the actual inventory count.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated the immediate repair of odometer/speedometer of all the motor vehicles of the municipality in order to ascertain the actual distance travelled for each motor vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Management discussed the matter with the concerned departments, instructed the drivers of the motor vehicles and the operators of heavy equipment to properly fill-up the trip tickets and facilitate the repair of the defective or malfunctioning odometers of some vehicles.
observations.reason: √ Post audit proves the stated action.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that collection of receivables must be intensified so that amount could be used to finance priority projects of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management had collected some of the receivables and will continue to collect the remaining balance. As at December 31, 2019, Due from Officers and Employees balance is P30,307.21.
observations.reason: √ Management is still in the process of collecting the amount due from officers and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee that will conduct inventory of the local roads and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.
We recommended that Management come up with the final list of properties for insurance with the GSIS and comply with the provisions of The Property Insurance Law.
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations to effect the legality of the procurement process.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Ensure compliance of the aforementioned provision of the Government Procurement Manual Volume 1, and
Evaluate the necessity of hiring additional work force to augment the personnel in the BAC Secretariat.

We recommended that management strictly comply with Section 54.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 76-41 to ensure transparency, economy and efficiency in all its procurement activities.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and BIR Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13, 2014 on the prescribed documentary requirements to support transactions on payment of purchases;

We recommended that Management refrain from paying registration fees directly to the hotel venue and require official receipts from the sponsoring agency or group for complete documentation of government expenditures.
We recommended that management observe the above provision of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA. 9184 on the coverage of guarantee bonds in lieu of retention money to assure the Government of the indemnity.
We recommended that Management direct the Supply Officer to prepare a RPCPPE by type as provided in the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units and submit the same to COA office for verification.

We recommended that management post every Notice of Award of procurement in the PhilGEPS website within the period provided by law to achieve transparency and to avoid any doubts on the propriety of bidding activity.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
The Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO), together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, immediately look into the unimplemented projects under the 20% Development Fund and determine causes of their non-implementation. Formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement development projects. Consider formulating a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented projects;
The offices concerned vigorously pursue the completion of the projects to be implemented the soonest time possible in order to achieve the objectives of the said projects in providing socio-economic benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
The Municipal Engineering Office improve the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of the Program of Work of unimplemented projects.
The MPDO enhance monitoring of implementation of projects in coordination with the implementing offices and to submit regularly status reports of implementation to the Municipal Mayor for his information so that appropriate measures can be instituted to obtain optimal utilization of fund.

We recommended the management to conduct a complete physical inventory of the assets of the municipality annually and a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted to our Office within the Deadline.
observations.managementAction: Management already submitted the Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to COA Office.
observations.reason: Upon verification, it was observed that the format of the submitted report was not in conformity with NGAS Manual for LGUs. A separate audit finding was issued to management and incorporated in this AAR as finding no. 10 for the deficiency noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO Designate to prepare, consolidate and submit the RIS to the Municipal Account which shall be the basis of the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher to record the issuances of inventories.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have received RIS for some of the inventories and shall continue to coordinate with the concerned offices and personnel for the unsubmitted RIS.
observations.reason: Adjusting entries were already taken by the Accounting office for the received RIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions to ensure transparency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Management have studied the identified transactions and have made the necessary adjustments and have given instructions/guidelines to the concerned Department and to all offices to properly observe the procurement process and executions of contract.
observations.reason: Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly follow the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to attain transparency in the conduct of the procurement.
observations.managementAction: The BAC have complied with the recommendations of the COA and the provisions of the revised IRR if RA 9184.
observations.reason: Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to adhere with Section 23.4.1.3 and to with the Statement of Single Largest Completed Contract with items similar with the contract procured to prevent suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management have adhered to the recommendations of COA and have adjusted the parameters
observations.reason: Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to adhere to section 25.2b Item viii (3) of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management have partially complied with the recommendations of COA and corrected the identified documents.
observations.reason: Auditorial Contract Review Analysis conducted on the Infrastructure projects of the Agency revealed that the attached contractor’s list of major equipment units for some projects were still not fully supported.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that all offices shall prepare and submit Project Procurement Management Plan to the BAC Secretariat for them to prepare the consolidated Annual Procurement Plan yearly and so that all procurement on goods, services and infrastructure projects will be properly planned.
observations.managementAction: Management have submitted their Annual Procurement Plan to COA Office.
observations.reason: Upon verification of the report submitted, it was noted that not all PPMPs were consolidated and the items included in the APP were not properly identified. It was also observed during the review of Purchase Requests that these were not consolidated resulting in piecemeal purchases. A separate audit finding was issued and incorporated in this AAR as finding no. 5 for the deficiency noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to adhere to Section 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave and CSC Resolution No. 000034.
observations.managementAction: The Management have adjusted the records and now adhering to the guidelines provided in the Omnibus Rules on Leave and CSC Resolution No. 000034.
observations.reason: Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management submit to our office all suspension orders or any documents that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned supplier, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the liquidated damages based on the provision cited in the foregoing.
observations.managementAction: The Management together with the BAC are now implementing the recommendation and continuously reminding the suppliers and creditors to observe the proper execution of the contracts.
observations.reason: Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to adhere with the requirements of the aforementioned provision to be used a basis for the verification of items purchased for inspection of deliveries and to determine the reasonableness of its price.
observations.managementAction: The municipal government have implemented the recommendations of COA. The BAC and the Municipal Accountant are now monitoring the proper implementations of the recommendation.
observations.reason: Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the management to trace from the related documents to identify the name of the beneficiaries of the various capital assistance and to exert extra effort in collecting the funds lent out by sending demand letters to concerned persons so other bona fide beneficiaries within the municipality could also avail of the program.
observations.managementAction: Management have collected some of the receivables and shall continue to collect the remaining balance. Request for the write-off of some of the accounts are also being considered upon the establishment of valid and acceptable reasons.
observations.reason: Collection is still in the process. Management is exerting efforts to trace from the related documents to identify the name of the beneficiaries of the various capital assistance.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated the management to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the assets of the municipality, reconcile the balances with the accounting records and exert all efforts in locating the documents needed to make the necessary adjustments in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Management commented that they will comply with the recommendations to conduct inventory count of our properties including our livestock and will continue to locate the records of those properties (Work/Zoo Animals and Breeding Stocks) and make the necessary adjustments and update our records.

Management asked an ample time to prepare the Inventory Report make the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: No action taken yet by management.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated the immediate repair of odometer/speedometer of all the motor vehicles of the municipality in order to ascertain the actual distance travelled for each motor vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Management discussed the matter with the concerned departments, we have instructed the drivers of the motor vehicles and the operators of heavy equipment to properly fill-up the trip tickets and facilitate the repair of the defective or malfunctioning odometers of some vehicles.
observations.reason: The odometer reading of some trip tickets of municipal vehicles were not properly filled-up.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management comply with the provisions of EO No. 80 on the granting of the P 5,000.00 PEI per municipal official and employee and require them to refund the excess amount of P 13,000.00 each.
observations.managementAction:
For the disallowed excess amount of                                                                                                            P 13,000.00, management have filed an Appeal/ Motion for Reconsideration to the COA Commission Proper.

However, management is requiring the retiring or separating employees from the service to pay the amount pending the result/decision of the Appeal.
observations.reason: Management is now granting the PEI amounting within the authorized rates, or P 5,000 since 2015.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that collection of receivables must be intensified so that amount could be used to finance priority projects of the municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Management have already collected some of the receivables and we shall continue to collect the remaining balance.

Management also commented that they might propose for the write-off of some of the accounts in the future after we have established valid and acceptable reasons to propose such.
observations.reason: Collection is still in the process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to conduct a complete physical
inventory of the assets of the municipality annually and a Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted
to our Office within the deadline.

We recommended the GSO Designate to prepare, consolidate and submit
the RIS to the Municipal Accountant which shall be the basis of the preparation
of the Journal Entry Voucher to record the issuances of inventories.

We recommended the management to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions
to ensure transparency in the procurement process.

We recommended the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly follow the
provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to attain transparency in the
conduct of the procurement.

We recommended the management to adhere with Section 23.4.1.3 and to
submit the Statement of Single Largest Completed Contract with items similar
with the contract procured to prevent suspension in audit.

We recommended the management to adhere to Section 25.2b Item viii (3)
of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We reiterated that all offices shall prepare and submit Project
Procurement Management Plan to the BAC Secretariat for them to prepare the
consolidated Annual Procurement Plan yearly and so that all procurement on
goods, services and infrastructure projects will be properly planned.

We recommended the management to adhere to Section 23 of the Omnibus
Rules on Leave and CSC Resolution No. 000034.

We recommended that the management submit to our office all suspension
orders or any documents that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated
damages to the concerned supplier, otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant
to compute the liquidated damages based on the provisions cited in the foregoing.

We recommended the management to adhere with the requirements of the
aforementioned provision to be used a basis for the verification of items
purchased for inspection of deliveries and to determine the reasonableness of its
price.

We have recommended the management to conduct a complete physical inventory of the assets of the municipality annually and a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted to our Office within the deadline.

observations.reason: The lacking Tax Clearances of suppliers were already submitted in our office.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated the management to require all offices to prepare and submit a complete Project Procurement Management Plan to the BAC Secretariat for them to prepare the consolidated Annual Procurement Plan yearly and so that all procurement on goods, services and infrastructure projects will be properly planned.

observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 7, page 68 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to review all claims for traveling expenses in order to avoid overpayment.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 complied with our recommendation.
observations.reason: Per diems of employees were paid in accordance with the authorized rates.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated the immediate repair of odometer/speedometer of all the motor vehicles of the municipality in order to ascertain the actual distance travelled for each motor vehicle.

observations.reason: The odometer reading of some trip tickets of municipal vehicles were not properly filled-up.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 complied with the Team. We are now requiring the Waste Materials Report as supporting documents of our Disbursement Vouchers for the payment of materials/parts that have been replaced and disposed.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Office have submitted the lacking documents of the listed disbursement vouchers.
