observations.recommendation: We have recommended that subsidies or funds utilized and given to the organizations stated above be supported with the required documents in order to validate the authenticity of their programs/projects in line with government thrust. Otherwise this will be subject to suspension and eventually disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 complied with the recommendations of the COA Audit Team and have explained the purpose/s of the financial assistance.
observations.reason: Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended for the proper classification of budgetary and expense accounts in conformity with the revised chart of accounts for local government units, particularly those expenditures which should not be classified as Other MOOE to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: 2016 complied with the COA Audit Team.
observations.reason: Management have provided the budget for the expenditures in the following year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that in the granting of financial assistance to various persons, the disbursement vouchers should always be supported by documents as required by the DSWD Office in order to ascertain that such assistance were extended to qualified persons only. Otherwise this will be subject to suspension and eventually to disallowance in audit for not supporting the said transactions.
observations.managementAction: 2015 management have complied with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management comply with the provisions of EO No. 80 on the granting of the P 5,000.00 PEI per municipal official and employee and require them to refund the excess amount of P 13,000.00 each.
observations.managementAction: For the disallowed excess amount of P 13,000, management have filed an Appeal/Motion for Reconsideration to the COA Commission Proper. However, management is requiring the retiring or separating employees from the service to pay the amount pending the result/decision of the Appeal.
observations.reason: Management is now granting the PEI amounting P 5,000 since 2015.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that collection of future receivables must be intensified so that amount could be used to finance priority projects of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: 2011 already collected some of the receivables and shall continue to collect the remaining balance. Management also might propose for the write-off of some of the accounts in the future after we have established valid and acceptable reasons to propose such.
observations.reason: Collection is still in process.

observations.recommendation: Determine those receivables deemed uncollectible so that management could request authority to write-off the same from the COA Adjudication and Settlement Board for each account of P1M and below pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2003-02 dated January 30, 2003.
observations.managementAction: 2007 commented that they shall continue to collect the remaining balance of the SEA Loans. Management also commented that they might propose for the write-off of some of the accounts in the future after we have established valid and acceptable reasons to propose such.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 11, page 72 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to conduct a complete physical inventory of the assets of the municipality annually and a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted to our Office within the deadline.

We have reiterated the management to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the assets of the municipality, reconcile the balances with the accounting records and exert all efforts in locating the documents needed to make the necessary adjustments in the financial statements.

We have recommended the BAC to submit the lacking documents immediately to our Office. Moreover, for succeeding transactions, we advise the management to ensure that all claims shall be supported with complete documents to avoid suspensions in audit.

We have reiterated the management to require all offices to prepare and submit a complete Project Procurement Management Plan to the BAC Secretariat for them to prepare the consolidated Annual Procurement Plan yearly and so that all procurement on goods, services and infrastructure projects will be properly planned.
We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to review all claims for traveling expenses in order to avoid overpayment.
We have reiterated the immediate repair of odometer/speedometer of all the motor vehicles of the municipality in order to ascertain the actual distance travelled for each motor vehicle.

We have recommended the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to require the attachment of the Waste Materials Report in all payments for repairs and maintenance of vehicles and similar nature as part of the supporting documents.
We have recommended the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly follow the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to avoid suspensions or disallowance in audit.
We have recommended the management to limit the grant of financial assistance only to those allowable by law.

We have recommended the management to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the assets of the municipality, reconcile the balances with the accounting records and exert all efforts in locating the documents needed to make the necessary adjustments in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Conducted physical count of all assets and submitted the report to the COA.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2, page 54 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Accounting Office to ensure proper and complete documentation of all disbursement to avoid possible suspensions in audit.
observations.managementAction: Consulted with the management and the BAC to make complete documentation of transactions.
observations.reason: Some disbursement vouchers still lack complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended for the proper classification of budgetary and expense accounts in conformity with the revised chart of accounts for local government units, particularly those expenditures which should not be classified as Other MOOE to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management have classified most of the budgetary and expense accounts. Some of the needed accounts were not included in the 2016 Annual Budget due to the transition period for the new accounts in the PPSAS. Proper expense accounts were included for 2017.
observations.reason: Proper classification of budgetary and expense accounts was made for the current year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the concerned officials designated for the acceptance and inspection of deliveries to ensure completeness of delivery and conformity with the specifications stated in the purchase order/contract.

observations.reason: Management complied with our recommendation.

observations.managementAction: The Department Heads and the BAC have prepared the APP for 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that in the allocation of the proceeds of the SEF, the Local School Board should consider only those programs, projects and activities that are responsive to the purposes stated in Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The management have consulted with the members of the Local School Board and remove the items in the fund that are not in conformity with the purposes prescribed under RA 7160.
observations.reason: Management have complied with our recommendation.
